description: Search YouTube for learning topics or research evidence, get transcripts/captions from YouTube videos, optionally download the video as an MP4 plus a separate audio file, run local ASR fallback for captionless authorized media, and run restartable queue-based retrieval with sleep/retry handling for rate limits. Use when Codex needs to search YouTube from a query/results URL, transcribe one or many YouTube URLs, save caption text/SRT/JSON, inspect available caption languages, resume interrupted retrieval, build a research/evidence source workflow, run local Nemotron/NeMo ASR when captions are missing, or download YouTube media files with yt-dlp for videos the user is authorized to save.
do_not_use_when: The user wants to bypass access controls, paywalls, region gates, or age restrictions.
The user wants to download media they are not allowed to save.
examples: Use get-youtube-media to find transcripts for YouTube videos about agent skill marketplaces.
Use get-youtube-media to get captions from this YouTube URL and save them as text and SRT.
Use get-youtube-media to search YouTube for videos about MR datasets and summarize the best sources.
expanded_description: Use this skill when a user wants YouTube transcripts, captions, search results, local ASR fallback for captionless videos, or authorized media downloads for research, evidence gathering, learning, or repeatable source collection. It supports search queries, YouTube result URLs, direct video URLs, restartable queues, language selection, caption formats, optional Nemotron/NeMo ASR, and optional video or audio downloads.
meta_description: Install the Get YouTube Media Skill for Codex to search YouTube, retrieve transcripts and captions, inspect caption languages, and support repeatable research workflows.
name: get-youtube-media
outputs: optional local ASR transcript for captionless videos
page_title: Get YouTube Media Skill - YouTube Transcript, Caption, and Research Workflow for Codex
permissions: network access to YouTube or YouTube Data API
local file writes for transcript, queue, caption, ASR, and optional media outputs
optional YOUTUBE_API_KEY for reliable license checks
optional local NeMo/PyTorch runtime and ffmpeg for ASR fallback
short_description: Search YouTube, retrieve transcripts and captions, run optional local ASR fallback, inspect caption languages, and download authorized media for research workflows.
tasks: get a transcript from a YouTube URL
save transcript text, SRT, or JSON
run local ASR for captionless authorized videos
download authorized YouTube video or audio
title: Get YouTube Media
use_when: The user needs YouTube transcripts, captions, or reusable video research artifacts.
The user wants to search YouTube and collect candidate sources for analysis.
The user is authorized to save requested media and asks for video or audio files.
warnings: External URL reference found in README.md
External URL reference found in SKILL.md
External URL reference found in scripts/youtube_media.py
